title: Syracuse hosted Healthy Housing 101 event for safe home practices
summary: Syracuse City Code Enforcement Department held the annual 'Healthy Housing 101' event to educate residents on safe home practices and encourage reporting of housing violations. Code officials emphasized that renters should not hesitate to contact the department about issues like lead, heat, water, and smoke detectors, with enforcement officers pledging support to address reported problems.

political_signal.description: Department proactively engaging residents on housing safety compliance and removing barriers to complaint filing. Messaging emphasizes partnership ('we'll fight for you') to build trust and increase reporting of code violations.

processing_notes: Article date discrepancy: headline shows 2025-06-26 but byline shows 'Wed, June 25, 2025' — used article header date as authoritative
No mechanical extractions validated; Law Department reference in instructions appears to be template artifact
Event framing suggests constituent engagement and transparency priority for CIO innovation initiatives
Potential connection to 311 service ecosystem if complaint intake/routing documented separately
